reasoning: SGA has scored 30+ in 7 of his last 10 games with a low of 20 pts, averaging ~32 pts over that stretch. The Knicks are a mid-tier defensive team and SGA has consistently produced elite scoring outputs regardless of opponent. His floor is exceptionally high given his usage rate and shot creation ability.

SGA has recorded 9, 13, 8, 9, 4, 6, 11, 4, 5, and 3 assists in his last 10 games, averaging roughly 7.2 AST. He hits 6+ assists in 6 of 10 recent games. The Knicks game should be competitive and high-usage for SGA as the primary ball-handler.

Brunson has scored 26, 26, 24, 27, 20, 19, 20, 33, 8, and 40 in his last 10 games, exceeding 21.5 in 8 of 10. His recent form is elite and he's the primary offensive engine at home. Even in slower games he typically crosses this threshold.

Brunson has recorded 10, 7, 4, 9, 6, 8, 10, 4, 3, and 8 assists in his last 10 games, hitting 6+ in 7 of 10. As the primary point guard for the Knicks at home against OKC, his playmaking role should remain elevated.

KAT has recorded 12, 12, 14, 13, 7, 11, 7, 11, 11, 14, and 10 rebounds in his last 10 games, exceeding 9.5 in 8 of 10. OKC's frontcourt matchup with Holmgren/Hartenstein could lead to active rebounding battles that favor KAT's positioning.

Holmgren has grabbed 11, 11, 9, 21, 9, 15, 7, 13, 4, 10, and 14 rebounds in his last 10 games, exceeding 8.5 in 8 of 10. His rebounding has been exceptional recently with a 21-rebound outlier, and the Knicks' interior gives him ample opportunities.

Holmgren has scored 12, 12, 19, 15, 7, 17, 15, 16, 13, 13, and 17 pts in last 10 games, hitting 13+ in 7 of 10. His offensive role is consistent as OKC's second option and the NY frontcourt matchup is manageable for his skill set.

Maxey has scored 21, 21, 33, 28, 32, 39, 27, 28, 32, 30, and 29 in his last 10 games, exceeding 26.5 in 9 of 10 with only one miss at 21. With Embiid and George out, Maxey is the clear #1 option and Utah is missing Markkanen, Kessler, Nurkic, and Williams — creating a weak defensive shell.

Maxey has recorded 2, 2, 6, 11, 8, 8, 7, 3, 2, 4, and 6 assists in his last 10 games, hitting 6+ in 6 of 10. With Embiid and George out, Maxey's playmaking burden increases significantly as the primary ball-handler for PHI.

With Tatum out, Brown becomes the clear primary option for Boston and has scored 27, 28, 23, 32, 23, 24, 26, 29 in his 8 active games (last 10 includes 2 DNPs). He hit 23+ in 7 of 8 active appearances. Charlotte ranks among the worst defensive teams in the league and this is a home game for Brown.

Brown has recorded 8, 7, 11, 8, 15, 4, 2, and 7 rebounds in his 8 active recent games, hitting 7+ in 5 of 8. With Tatum absent, Brown's role expands on both ends and Charlotte's soft interior should enable additional rebounding opportunities.

White has scored 18, 21, 12, 20, 22, 12, 10, 9, 19, and 21 in his last 10 games, exceeding 14.5 in 7 of 10. With Tatum out, White's usage elevates further as a secondary scorer and Charlotte ranks among the weakest defensive teams in the league.

White has recorded 9, 8, 7, 3, 8, 8, 8, 9, 4, and 5 assists in his last 10 games, hitting 6+ in 7 of 10. With Tatum out, White's playmaking role expands and Charlotte's defense should allow for open looks and easy assists.

White has made 4, 5, 4, 4, 4, 2, 0, 2, 3, and 4 threes in his last 10 games, hitting 3+ in 7 of 10 and 4+ in 5 of 10. Charlotte allows the most threes in the league and White's recent volume and accuracy have been exceptional.

Pritchard has scored 25, 0, 22, 3, 8, 30, 26, 26, 6, and 24 in his last 10 games, exceeding 14.5 in 6 of 10 with variance driven by his shot-making. With Tatum out, Boston will rely more on bench scoring and Charlotte is an extremely weak defensive team that will allow open three-point looks.

LaMelo has recorded 9, 9, 8, 8, 7, 7, 6, 7, 6, 9, and 4 assists in his last 10 games, hitting 7+ in 8 of 10 and 6+ in 9 of 10. His playmaking floor is very consistent and even against Boston's defense his assist volume should remain high as the primary facilitator.

LaMelo has made 4, 4, 2, 3, 4, 10, 2, 1, 7, 4, and 2 threes in his last 10 games, hitting 3+ in 7 of 10. His three-point volume is extremely high and even against Boston's defense his shot creation ability should produce at least 3 makes.

Miller has scored 17, 17, 26, 33, 23, 22, 18, 17, 31, 24, and 16 in his last 10 games, hitting 20+ in 6 of 10. With Tatum out, Boston's defense loses its best wing defender and Miller's scoring floor has been very consistent as CHA's leading scorer.

Miller has made 2, 2, 6, 6, 5, 5, 4, 1, 5, 4, and 3 threes in his last 10 games, hitting 4+ in 7 of 10. His three-point volume is elite and without Tatum guarding him, the defensive coverage weakens significantly for Boston.

Knueppel has scored 13, 13, 10, 28, 21, 28, 33, 15, 18, 20, and 23 in his last 10 games, hitting 17+ in 7 of 10. As a key secondary scorer for Charlotte, he benefits from Tatum's absence and has shown a high ceiling in recent games.

Giannis has scored 19, 22, 19, 21, 21, 25, 31, 21, 34, and 37 in his last 10 games, exceeding 19.5 in 9 of 10. Atlanta's defense has struggled against elite bigs and Giannis playing at home should produce a high-usage, high-efficiency performance.

Giannis has recorded 11, 13, 14, 17, 5, 8, 8, 6, 10, and 11 rebounds in his last 10 games, hitting 10+ in 5 of 10 and exceeding 9.5 in 6 of 10. Atlanta's frontcourt is not built to limit his rebounding and he's an elite rebounder for his position.

Johnson has grabbed 8, 3, 12, 16, 10, 13, 9, 16, 11, and 12 rebounds in his last 10 games, hitting 10+ in 7 of 10. His rebounding volume is excellent and Milwaukee's frontcourt with Prince out allows Johnson to work freely on the glass.

Johnson has scored 8, 5, 26, 16, 32, 19, 31, 22, 29, and 33 in his last 10 games, hitting 15+ in 7 of 10. He is Atlanta's primary scoring option and despite a tough matchup with Giannis, his usage rate ensures enough opportunities to surpass this threshold.

Kawhi has scored 23, 23, 37, 31, 23, 27, 24, 41, 31, and 25 in his last 10 games, exceeding 22.5 in all 10. His scoring floor is elite and with Haliburton questionable and Zubac out for Indiana, the matchup heavily favors the Clippers' offensive output.

Kawhi has grabbed 8, 3, 8, 4, 4, 12, 8, 8, 9, and 7 rebounds in his last 10 games, hitting 6+ in 7 of 10. Without Zubac for Indiana and playing at home, Kawhi should have ample rebounding opportunities against a shorthanded Pacers frontcourt.

Bailey has scored 18, 13, 23, 4, 20, 15, 13, 16, 10, and 20 in his last 10 games, hitting 14+ in 7 of 10. PHI is missing Embiid and George creating a weak defensive unit, and Utah is also shorthanded giving Bailey elevated usage and opportunities to score.

Bailey has grabbed 4, 8, 4, 8, 6, 8, 4, 8, 3, and 6 rebounds in his last 10 games, hitting 6+ in 6 of 10. With multiple Utah bigs missing (Kessler, Nurkic OFS), Bailey will likely see increased minutes and rebounding responsibility.
